MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Continued To Mar-30-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Discovery Response To Demand For Inspection (Set Two), Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Yaelle E. Gurel In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICHAEL TOY's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Discovery Response To Demand For Inspection (Set Two), Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion to compel plaintiff's further responses to defendant's second set of inspection demands is denied. The court orders defendant to pay $500 in sanctions to the court within 30 days and to file notice that defendant has complied.

The court denies the motion because it is procedurally defective. First, defendant failed to meet and confer. Defendant acknowledges that plaintiff served responses to the discovery; defendant takes issue with the content of the responses and that they included only objections. (Gurel Decl., para. 9.) Defendant therefore seeks further responses and brings this motion under Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.310, subdivision (a)(3). Such a motion must be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration. (Id, sec. 2031.310, subd. (b)(2).) Defendant provides no evidence of informal resolution efforts. That trial was approaching at the time the document demand was made is a problem of defendant's own making.

Second, defendant fails to include either a separate statement or plaintiff's discovery responses. The court cannot rule on the merits of objections that it does not have before it. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (b)(3).)

Defendant seeks sanctions; plaintiff does not. The court nonetheless orders defendant to pay $500 to the court within 30 days for failing to meet and confer where required. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h); id., sec. 2023.010, subd. (i).) Defendant is further ordered to file a notice with the court once it has complied with the sanction order. If defendant contests the sanctions portion of the tentative ruling, the court will put over the matter for further hearing on the sanction to permit defendant notice.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to deliver to Dept. 301 a paper copy of the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Discovery Response To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection (Sets One); Request For Monetary Sanctions, Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Yaelle E. Gurel In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL TOY's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Discovery Response To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection (Sets One); Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant Michael Toy's motion to compel further discovery responses is denied as moot; plaintiff Brandon Herrera Aguirre has now served responses. Toy's motion for sanctions is granted.

Discovery is intended to be self-executing. Toy granted an extension on May 2, 2025, and received no responses. Toy followed up on June 11, 2025, and June 13, 2025, without response. This motion was filed on July 28, 2025 and Herrera Aguirre's counsel provided responses to the discovery requests the same day. On this record, the court concludes that Herrera Aguirre engaged in a misuse of the discovery process by failing to submit to an authorized method of discovery and that responses were served only because the motion was filed. The court orders plaintiff Herrera Aguirre to pay $660 to Toy within 30 days of entry of this order. (See Rule of Court 3.1348(a); Code Civ. Proc. 2023.010, subd. (d).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in commpliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
